cover.title: AI reasoning has a cliff
cover.sub: Apple's 2025 'Illusion of Thinking' tested frontier reasoning models on puzzles of rising complexity. Accuracy fell off.

slides.title: near-zero past a threshold
slides.body: Past a complexity threshold, accuracy collapsed to almost nothing. A sharp cliff, not a gentle decline.

slides.title: even handed the algorithm
slides.body: Given the exact steps to follow, the models still fell apart on hard instances. It isn't a knowledge gap.

slides.title: three regimes, not one
slides.body: Plain models win simple tasks, reasoning models win medium, both collapse on hard. Match the tool to the complexity.

slides.title: your hardest problems are the edge
slides.body: The critics have a point about the puzzle setup. The practical lesson survives: above a complexity line, agents need a human operator.

cta.title: find your complexity line
cta.sub: A straight read of your agent setup in your real repo, sources and method included. The prioritized plan is yours to keep.
